page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.sn2ne.4xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 16 dedicated physical cores running on an Intel Xeon E5-2682v4 processor clocked at 2.5 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, the processor includes 35 MB of L3 cache and supports instruction sets such as AVX, AVX512F, AES, and FMA. The instance is configured with 64.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, relying on network-attached storage and featuring a baseline network bandwidth of 3 Gbps.

In benchmark evaluations, the ecs.sn2ne.4xlarge demonstrates moderate multi-threaded performance. While single-core performance is relatively weak, as shown by a stress-ng single-core score of 1107.49 and a poor gzip compression rate of 19.07 GB/sec, the instance scales effectively under multi-threaded workloads. The stress-ng multi-core CPU performance reaches an average tier of 11751.7, and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression registers at 57.21 GB/sec. Memory bandwidth is stable, with read-only large block speeds at 77.7 GB/sec and write speeds at 59.92 GB/sec.

The hardware profile offers a balanced resource density suitable for standard enterprise applications. Because it utilizes dedicated physical cores without hyperthreading, it avoids resource contention issues common in shared-core environments, though it lacks the raw single-thread speed of newer CPU generations. This makes it a qualitatively cost-efficient option for workloads that require steady, predictable CPU allocation rather than peak burst performance.

The ecs.sn2ne.4xlarge is well-suited for general-purpose workloads, including static web serving, where it achieves a throughput of 7.81 GB/sec, and database operations, supported by a PassMark database score of 4395.88 and Redis SET performance of 11.29 million operations per second. It can also handle lightweight machine learning tasks, such as prompt processing and text generation for small 135M parameter models, though performance drops significantly on larger models.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.sn2ne.4xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 16 dedicated physical cores on an Intel Xeon E5-2682v4 processor clocked at 2.5 GHz. It is equipped with 64.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 4.0 GB per core, but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Built on the x86_64 architecture, the instance supports AVX and AVX512F instructions. Benchmark results indicate moderate multi-core capabilities, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 11751.7 and a PassMark CPU score of 13262.56, though single-core performance is comparatively weak. With a baseline network bandwidth of 3 Gbps, this instance is designed for predictable, non-burstable workloads. It is best suited for general-purpose applications, static web serving, multi-threaded decompression, and standard database operations.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.sn2ne.4xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 16 dedicated Intel Xeon cores, 64 GB RAM, and 3 Gbps bandwidth, offering balanced multi-core performance for databases and web serving.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.sn2ne.4xlarge features 16 dedicated Intel Xeon cores and 64 GB RAM, delivering balanced performance for databases and web serving.
tagline: A dedicated multi-core virtual server offering balanced memory and compute resources for predictable enterprise workloads and database operations.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated physical Intel Xeon E5-2682v4 cores at 2.5 GHz
64.0 GB of system memory providing 4.0 GB per core
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Optimized for static web serving and standard database operations
